title: Visiting the Sick
arabic: لَا بَأْسَ طَهُورٌ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ
transliteration: La ba'sa, tahurun in sha' Allah.
translation: No harm. It is a purification, if Allah wills.
whenToRecite: When visiting someone who is ill.

title: Ruqyah for the Sick
arabic: أَذْهِبِ الْبَأْسَ رَبَّ النَّاسِ، اشْفِ وَأَنْتَ الشَّافِي، لَا شِفَاءَ إِلَّا شِفَاؤُكَ، شِفَاءً لَا يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
transliteration: Adhhibil-ba'sa Rabban-nas, ishfi wa Antash-Shafi, la shifa'a illa shifa'uk, shifa'an la yughadiru saqama.
translation: Remove the suffering, Lord of mankind. Grant healing, for You are the Healer. There is no healing except Your healing, a healing that leaves no illness behind.
whenToRecite: When making ruqyah for a sick person.

title: For Pain in the Body
whenToRecite: Place your hand on the area of pain and say this three times.

title: Supplication for Pain
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ
transliteration: A'udhu billahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhir.
translation: I seek refuge in Allah and His Power from the evil of what I find and what I fear.
whenToRecite: After saying 'Bismillah' three times, repeat this seven times.

title: At the Time of Death
translation: There is no deity worthy of worship except Allah.
whenToRecite: Prompt the dying person gently with these words.

title: Upon Hearing of a Death
transliteration: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un.
translation: Surely we belong to Allah and to Him we shall return.
whenToRecite: Upon hearing the news of someone's death.

title: Entering the Graveyard
arabic: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الدِّيَارِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ بِكُمْ لَلَاحِقُونَ، نَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ لَنَا وَلَكُمُ الْعَافِيَةَ
transliteration: As-salamu 'alaykum ahlad-diyari minal-mu'minina wal-muslimin, wa inna in sha' Allahu bikum lalaḥiqun, nas'alullaha lana wa lakumul-'afiyah.
translation: Peace be upon you, dwellers of these homes, believers and Muslims. We will, if Allah wills, join you. We ask Allah to grant us and you well-being.

title: Supplication for the Deceased
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ، وَعَافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْهُ
translation: O Allah, forgive him, have mercy on him, grant him well-being and pardon him.
whenToRecite: When making dua for a deceased Muslim.

title: Consoling the Bereaved
arabic: إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا أَخَذَ، وَلَهُ مَا أَعْطَى، وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ بِأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى، فَلْتَصْبِرْ وَلْتَحْتَسِبْ
transliteration: Inna lillahi ma akhadh, wa lahu ma a'ta, wa kullu shay'in 'indahu bi ajalin musamma, faltasbir waltahtasib.
translation: Indeed, to Allah belongs what He takes and what He gives, and everything with Him has an appointed time. So be patient and seek your reward from Allah.
whenToRecite: To console someone who has lost a loved one.
